content.text: If you try to escape me, I'll punish you.

The Fortress attacks like a normal character — there are zero offense amplifiers anywhere in this tree. Its entire budget is spent on reactions and space. The measure of a Fortress is not damage dealt and not damage soaked: it is ground the enemy didn't gain.
Two branches hold it. Bastion is the point: nothing gets past me. Warden is the zone: you don't get to choose your target.
Perimeter integrity is a deliverable. Ground surrendered will be itemized on the after-action invoice.

content.stat_lean: None printed — the budget here is RP, and RP is banked AP (see Turn, AP & Reactions).
content.theme: Movement denial: pins, entry triggers, and a toll on every band.
content.design: The measure is ground the enemy didn't gain. Zero offense amplifiers — the whole budget buys reactions and space.

content.text: The Engine — Buying What Already Exists

Denial needs no new subsystem. Reaction attacks already cost weapon AP in RP and already trigger on enemy movement (Turn, AP & Reactions). The Fortress simply buys more RP, cheaper reactions, broader triggers — and the one new verb, the pin: a reaction attack that lands on a moving enemy can end their movement where you caught them.

A normal character is not a wall; everything walks past. A Tier 2 Fortress at a doorway holds half of an entire squad, taxes the rest roughly nine enemy-rounds, and charges damage and a pin per crossing attempt.

The honest governor: the wall is RP-bound. Enough crossers exhaust the budget and the late ones leak through — by design. Choke points are the Fortress's natural habitat: the narrower the door, the further the budget stretches.
Fortress pays in tempo. RP is banked AP — every reaction held in reserve is an action you didn't take on your own turn. The toll booth is funded by your initiative.

Against the Shadow, the door wins: hit-and-run protection keys to after the hit, and Gatekeeper collects before it. The Fortress wins the Bumper Duel at the threshold. Against the Dancer, Ghost Step slips the free-shift triggers and The Wall deletes the free shift entirely — that arms race is intended and printed in both trees.
Counterplay, Printed
Range — the wall holds ground, not airspace.
Suppression — pin the pinner; the zone collapses while you're suppressed.
Shove and grapple — move the wall instead of passing it. Grapnel Hands is the arms race.
Volume — RP exhausts; the late crosser walks.
Bosses — poise tiers grant pin resistance.
Paying the toll — sometimes the damage and the pin are worth the position, and a smart enemy knows it.
The Catalog
Install costs follow the standard ladder (Trees Overview). Tier 5 means choosing ten of the catalog below. Capstones are marked.

augments.name: Threat Lattice
augments.effect: +1 RP. The lattice is watching.

content.text: Bastion Branch — The Point

augments.name: Brace
augments.effect: Your melee reaction attacks cost 1 less RP (floor 1). Even the maul learns to wait.

augments.name: Lockdown Servos
augments.effect: When your reaction attack hits a moving enemy, their movement ends — pinned where you caught them.

augments.name: Gatekeeper
augments.effect: Your reaction triggers include enemies entering your reach, not just leaving it. The toll runs both directions.

augments.name: Subdermal Plating
augments.effect: Personal Absorb 1, under and independent of armor.

augments.name: Toll Collector
augments.effect: Your first reaction attack each round costs 0 RP.

augments.name: The Wall
augments.effect: Capstone. Enemies in your Adjacent cluster lose the free Adjacent-to-Close shift, and leaving your reach costs +1 AP per band. Your RP bonus rises to +3 — the Fortress ceiling.

content.text: Warden Branch — The Zone

augments.name: Dread Presence
augments.effect: Enemies in your Adjacent cluster pay +1 AP on their first band move each turn. You are difficult terrain with opinions.

augments.name: Challenge Mark
augments.effect: 1 AP: mark an enemy in Close. While it is in your reach envelope, its attacks against anyone but you grant you a reaction attack at 1 less RP. Not mind control — consequences.

augments.name: Intercept
augments.effect: Reaction, 2 RP: when an ally in your Adjacent cluster takes a hit, the punch-through comes to you instead. Your armor, your problem.

augments.name: Grapnel Hands
augments.effect: Advantage on grapple and shove contests, and your grapples hold without re-rolling until the target wins a contest on their turn.

augments.name: War Cry
augments.effect: 3 AP, once per fight: every enemy in Close immediately moves its Challenge Mark to you or eats a reaction attack. The room learns who owns it.

augments.name: Warden's Court
augments.effect: Capstone. Your zone extends to Close: allies inside it gain +1 EV, and Dread Presence taxes all movement in the zone, not just the first band.

content.text: Catalog Additions
The rest of the catalog, by tier.

augments.name: Anchor Spikes
augments.effect: Advantage on contests resisting shoves and grapples.

augments.name: Magnetized Soles
augments.effect: Difficult terrain doesn't slow you.

augments.name: Kinetic Dampeners
augments.effect: Forced movement against you is reduced by 1 band.

augments.name: Echo Plate
augments.effect: When you Intercept, the attacker takes 1 direct damage.

augments.name: Bracer Lock
augments.effect: Enemies you have pinned have −1 EV until they escape.

augments.name: Watchman Optic
augments.effect: You cannot be flanked.

augments.name: Bulkhead Frame
augments.effect: Your worn armor gains +4 Armor HP. The shell clock, bought longer.

augments.name: Sentry Stims
augments.effect: +1 RP. Counts toward the Fortress +3 RP ceiling.

augments.name: Last Bastion
augments.effect: Alternate capstone. While you are conscious, allies in your zone cannot be pinned or force-moved. The wall has a family.

content.text: Reminder: contracted denial specialists are compensated for territory held, not casualties produced. Kill counts submitted for bonus consideration will be returned unread.
holding a door all night makes no highlight reel. it makes survivors. take the door.
